picks.narrative: The model prices LAA at 43.0% to win where the no-vig market implies 34.7%, a 9.0pp disagreement. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): SEA's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +194 still sits inside the playable range (to +156). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

This is value in the number more than the matchup — the model just makes TEX a better side than the price does.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on LAD by 4.4pp — 69.0% fair against a 63.2% price. Several smaller signals stack the same way — home field (~+1.0pp), pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.5pp) — with no single one dominating. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -182 still sits inside the playable range (to -185). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.

This is value in the number more than the matchup — the model just makes PIT a better side than the price does.

picks.narrative: The model sees COL as a 48.7% side; the market is pricing 44.8%, leaving a 4.2pp edge. The largest lift is home field (~+1.1pp): Home-park and 2026 home-game context add a modest edge for COL. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +125 still sits inside the playable range (to +124). The edge is the model pricing COL differently than the market rather than one of the marquee starter/lineup/bullpen buckets — worth taking at the number, not chasing past it.
